w:r.w:t: The information contained in this document is proprietary to CI Systems and may not be reproduced, used, or disclosed without its prior written consent.
This document describes the Acceptance Test Procedure (ATP) for the METS-
Collimator major components as well as for the integrated system.
The tests are defined and the detailed procedures are given in this document.
The system test results are summarized in the Acceptance Test Report (ATR).
Collimator performances are with respect to a standard laboratory environment:
The ATP refers to the following tests:
collimator contains the following major components:
system for its major component and verify
of all quality assurance (QA) documents, as listed
Verify that the mirrors’ interferograms are attached.
Verify that the focusing interferogram is attached.
Visually inspect the collimator mechanical structure for defects.
Verify that the target wheel is rotated freely and is controlled via the c
Verify that all the cables are as described in the ATR document.
Write Pass/Fail in the ATR document.
Inspect the following and verify its condition
Marking & Identification labels on system and each subsystem
the exit aperture of the collimator-Flir inspection
Target plates side towards the collimator
Mechanical integrity of all the targets (see target table), supplied target inspection report
Location of targets on the plates as defined in the target table in the ATR document
Write Pass/Fail in the ATR document.
Write Pass/Fail in the ATR document.
Verify that the EFL is according to CI requirements.
Calculate the FOV using the target plate diameter and the EFL as in manufacturer COT.
Record the test results and write Pass/Fail in the ATR document.
Calculates the transmission using the reflection factor of both mirrors @
Record the test results in the ATR document.
Verify that the diameter of the parabolic mirror is according to CI requirements.
Use a ruler or any other measuring device and measure the collimator’s clear aperture diameter.
Record the test results in the ATR document.
Verify that the target wheel is running free when motor power is disconnected.
otate the target wheel using the
verify the wheel is running free and stops according to command.
Verify the targets are set on the wheel as required by system specifications.
Record the test results in the ATR document.
The boresight between targets should be
Focus the autocollimator until a sharp image is achieved.
set the target wheel to a
Align the autocollimator until the center of the image is located at the center of the autocollimator’s cross reticule.
Rotate the target wheel to the next target.
Verify that the center of the target is located no more than
Repeat stage 3.7.1.3 & 3.7.1.4 for all the targets.
Record the test results in the ATR document.
is completed according to the procedure
C. Verify the unit reaches the desired temperature.
Record the test results in the ATR document.
Verify that the ATP for the SR-300N is completed according to the procedure
Write Pass/Fail in the ATR document Integrating Sphere
Inspect the following and verify its condition
Write Pass/Fail in the ATR document

focus stage travels from the BB Source to the Back Light. Make sure that during this travel there are no impacts and that the travel is smooth. Make sure no cables are tangled due to the source stage travel.
Source stage travels from the Back light to the
BB. Make sure that during this travel there are no impacts and that the travel is smooth. Make sure no cables are tangled due to the source stage travel.
Test radiometric offset according to the procedure and record the results
Requirement: The initiated self-test shall test the TESTER devices
Run the initiated self-test via the TESTER – CTE software.
Run the following tests: MRTD, MTF, NETD. SITF, Uniformity.
The following items are checked: SR
Verify that the test ends with Pass/Fail message for each item.
Write Pass/Fail in the ATR document
Cycles/mRad and mRad units calculations are based on a nominal effective focal length of
